cel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Celtic languages.
cell: noun. A single - room dwelling for a hermit.

cleome: noun. Any flowering plant in the genus Cleome.
clue: noun. A strand of yarn etc. as used to guide one through a labyrinth; something which points the way, a guide.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
come: verb. To move nearer to the point of perspective.

cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
cull: verb. To pick or take someone or something (from a larger group).
culm: noun. Waste coal, used as a poor quality fuel.
cum: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Cumeral.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.

locule: noun. A little hollow; a loculus.
luce: noun. The pike, Esox lucius, when fully grown.

mel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Melanau.
mell: verb. To say (something); to speak, to tell.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
mole: noun. A naevus, a pigmented, slightly raised, and sometimes hairy spot on the skin.
molecule: noun. The smallest particle of a specific element or compound that retains the chemical properties of that element or compound; two or more
moll: noun. A female companion of a gangster or other criminal, especially a former or current prostitute.
moue: noun. A pout, especially as expressing mock-annoyance or flirtatiousness.
mule: noun. The generally sterile male or female hybrid offspring of a male donkey and a female horse.
mull: verb. To work (over) mentally; to cogitate; to ruminate.

oleum: noun. A solution of sulfur trioxide in sulfuric acid.
